# Linkspector

> CLI tool that checks for broken hyperlinks in Markdown and AsciiDoc files. Uses Puppeteer headless Chrome as a fallback for JavaScript-rendered pages and bot-protected links.

## Usage

npx @umbrelladocs/linkspector check        # check with defaults
npx @umbrelladocs/linkspector check -j     # JSON (RDJSON) output for machine consumption
npx @umbrelladocs/linkspector check -q     # quiet mode, exit code only
npx @umbrelladocs/linkspector check -s     # show statistics table
npx @umbrelladocs/linkspector check -c path/to/config.yml  # custom config

## Configuration

Create `.linkspector.yml` in the project root:

```yaml
dirs:
  - .
  - docs/
excludedDirs:
  - ./node_modules
  - ./vendor
excludedFiles:
  - ./CHANGELOG.md
fileExtensions:
  - md
  - adoc
useGitIgnore: true
ignorePatterns:
  - pattern: '^https://example\.com/skip/.*$'
replacementPatterns:
  - pattern: "(https?://example\.com)/(\w+)/(\d+)"
    replacement: '$1/id/$3'
baseUrl: https://example.com
aliveStatusCodes:
  - 200
  - 204
httpHeaders:
  - url:
      - https://api.example.com
    headers:
      Authorization: ${AUTH_TOKEN}
followRedirects: true
timeout: 30000
retryCount: 3
modifiedFilesOnly: false
```

## Output

Exit code 0 means all links are valid. Non-zero means broken links were found.

With `-j`, outputs RDJSON (ReviewDog JSON) format with file paths, line numbers, column numbers, URLs, and HTTP status codes for each broken link.

## MCP Server

For native AI agent integration via Model Context Protocol, use the MCP server:

npm install -g @umbrelladocs/linkspector-mcp

Exposes two tools:
- `check_links` — full broken link report in RDJSON format
- `check_links_summary` — quick pass/fail with statistics

See https://github.com/UmbrellaDocs/linkspector-mcp for setup with Claude Desktop, Claude Code, VS Code, and Cursor.

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/UmbrellaDocs/linkspector
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@umbrelladocs/linkspector
- GitHub Action: https://github.com/UmbrellaDocs/action-linkspector
- MCP Server: https://github.com/UmbrellaDocs/linkspector-mcp
